abp-infrastructure/configure-audit-loggi.task: Automatically keep a change-history log for an entity — who modified it, when, and the old/new value of each changed property — without hand-writing that tracking in every service.

abp-infrastructure/encrypt-strings.task: Reversibly encrypt a sensitive text value before storing it and decrypt it back later, using the application's shared configuration so any instance can read it.

abp-infrastructure/generate-guids.task: Generate a database-friendly, sequentially-ordered identifier for a new entity instead of a fully random one, so inserts don't fragment the clustered index.

abp-infrastructure/handle-dates-and-time.task: Stamp an entity with the current time in a way that respects the application's configured time/UTC policy and stays testable, rather than reading the machine clock directly.

abp-infrastructure/manage-settings-and-f.task: Define a configurable value (like an SMTP host) with a default that admins can change at runtime and can override per tenant or per user, then read the effective value in code.

abp-infrastructure/manipulate-images.task: Resize an uploaded image to thumbnail dimensions and compress it, while keeping the code independent of any specific image-processing library.

abp-infrastructure/propagate-correlation.task: Read the identifier that ties together all log entries for the current request and include it on an outbound HTTP call to a downstream service.

abp-infrastructure/serialize-json.task: Serialize a payload object to a JSON string and later deserialize it back, in shared infrastructure code that should follow the application's JSON conventions.

abp-infrastructure/use-cancellation-toke.task: In a helper class that is not handed a cancellation token as a parameter, obtain the current operation's cancellation token (tied to the aborting web request) and honor it.

abp-infrastructure/use-interceptors-and-.task: Add reusable before-and-after behavior (for example, measuring and logging each call's execution time) around every call to a set of services, without editing each service's methods.

abp-data-access/apply-data-filters.task: In one operation, read the rows that were previously soft-deleted (normally hidden from queries), then make sure subsequent queries hide them again as usual.

abp-data-access/ef-core-integration.task: Set up the app's Entity Framework Core database context and register a custom repository for the Book entity that adds a 'list books by author id' query, keeping the domain project free of any EF Core references.

abp-data-access/handle-optimistic-concur.task: Protect an entity so that if two users load the same record and both save, the second save is rejected instead of silently overwriting; carry the version token through the read DTO and the update DTO.

abp-data-access/manage-units-of-work.task: In a console/background worker (no web request), run several repository writes so they all commit together on success and all roll back if any step throws.

abp-data-access/mongodb-integration.task: Set up the app's MongoDB data context and add a custom repository for the Book entity with a 'list books by author id' query, keeping the domain project free of MongoDB driver references.

abp-data-access/query-with-dapper.task: Add a repository method that runs a hand-written SQL query for a hot read path, reusing the app's current database connection and transaction so it sees uncommitted changes and rolls back with everything else.

abp-data-access/seed-application-data.task: On database setup, insert one default record only if that table/collection is empty, in a way that runs the same on both relational and document databases and is safe to re-run.

abp-data-access/use-abp-repositories.task: In an application service, return a paged, sorted list of an aggregate with its child collection eager-loaded, and run a filtered async LINQ query, without coupling the service to any specific database provider.

abp-files/manage-virtual-files.task: Embed a folder of static js/css assets into the assembly and read one of them at runtime by a virtual path, set up so another module can override a file by shipping one at the same path.

abp-files/store-blobs.task: Store a user's uploaded profile-picture bytes keyed by their user id and read them back later, using a storage backend (disk, database, or cloud) you can swap via configuration without changing this code; give this kind of file its own named container.

abp-multitenancy/configure-multi-tenancy.task: Make an entity's rows automatically isolated per tenant, then write a host-side method that counts a single specified tenant's rows by temporarily acting as that tenant.

abp-runtime/background-jobs-and-events.task: Run a cleanup routine on a fixed recurring interval that the framework manages, and separately react to a domain record being created by handling it in a decoupled handler instead of calling the reacting code inline.

abp-runtime/cache-entities.task: Cache a frequently looked-up entity by its id so repeated reads skip the database, and make the cached copy refresh automatically whenever that entity is updated or deleted.

abp-runtime/configure-app-urls.task: Build an absolute password-reset link to embed in an email, using the app's configured public root so it resolves correctly per tenant instead of hard-coding a host.

abp-runtime/distributed-caching-and-lock.task: Cache an expensive computed value so it is shared across all app instances, and guard a scheduled job so only one instance runs the critical section at a time in a multi-instance deployment.

abp-runtime/render-text-templates.task: Render a named text template (such as an email body) merged with a model object into a final string, with per-culture localized content.

abp-runtime/secure-web-requests.task: Add standard browser security response headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and a Content-Security-Policy) to every response across the app.

abp-runtime/send-emails.task: Send an HTML confirmation email to a user from a service, and offer a variant that queues the send so the web request does not block on SMTP.

abp-runtime/send-sms.task: Send an SMS verification code to a phone number from a service, keeping the sending code independent of the specific SMS gateway.

abp-runtime/use-hybrid-caching.task: Cache a hot typed value using both a fast local in-memory tier and a shared distributed tier, with protection against many concurrent misses all recomputing the same value.

abp-authentication/configure-dynamic-cla.task: Add a custom claim to every authenticated user's identity, recomputed from current data on each request so a change to it takes effect without forcing the user to sign in again.

abp-realtime/add-signalr-realtime.task: Add a real-time server-push messaging endpoint clients connect to, where the server-side handler can read the current authenticated user.

abp-microservices/design-module-and-serv.task: Have one microservice announce that a record's state changed so other services react to it asynchronously, and expose a small query one service can call on another synchronously, without sharing databases or domain classes.

abp-microservices/integrate-dapr-service.task: Route service-to-service calls and event pub/sub through a Dapr sidecar while keeping the existing application code, and secure the receive endpoints against arbitrary callers.

abp-module-development/application-servi.task: Implement a use-case service that accepts and returns data-transfer objects (never the entity itself) and is published as a REST endpoint automatically, without hand-writing a controller.

abp-module-development/build-crud-applic.task: Provide standard create/read/update/delete plus a filtered, sorted, paged list for an entity and its DTOs by reusing a framework base class instead of coding each operation by hand.

abp-module-development/create-plugin-mod.task: Load an add-on assembly from a folder at application startup so its services and pages register automatically, with no compile-time project reference.

abp-module-development/customize-applica.task: Add an extra persisted field to a pre-built user-management module's user as a real database column, expose it on that module's DTOs, and override one of the module's service methods, all without editing the module's source.

abp-module-development/define-applicatio.task: Create a reusable module unit that declares its dependencies on other modules, registers its own services, configures an options object, and runs startup logic when the application boots.

abp-module-development/extend-objects-wi.task: Add a runtime-defined extra field to your own entity and its DTO without declaring a CLR property for it, and persist that field as a real database column.

abp-module-development/handle-validation.task: Validate an incoming request object automatically, and on a broken business rule raise a domain error that maps to a proper HTTP status and a message that can be localized by an error code.

abp-module-development/localize-applicat.task: Set up application-wide translated text using per-culture files, a resource type you can inject to resolve strings in any layer, and a mapping so a business error's code resolves to a translation.

abp-module-development/map-objects-and-d.task: Convert entities to DTOs through a mapping abstraction so services don't depend on a concrete mapping library, and register the maps at the module level.

abp-module-development/model-domain-aggr.task: Model a business object with a generated GUID id, invariants enforced through methods rather than public setters, an immutable value object compared by its values, and a signal published when the object's state changes.

abp-module-development/register-and-repl.task: Swap in your own implementation for an existing service registration using the framework's registration conventions, exposing it under the same interface that consumers already inject.

abp-authorization/authorize-resources.task: Let a user act on one specific record they own or were granted (for example, edit this one document) rather than granting a blanket capability over every record, and check that per-instance access in code.

abp-authorization/permissions-and-author.task: Define a named capability, protect a service method so only users granted it can call it, and also check whether the current user holds that capability programmatically.

abp-api/configure-swagger-openapi.task: Add an interactive Swagger/OpenAPI UI to the API host with an Authorize button that logs the user in against the app's OAuth/OpenID Connect server.

abp-api/consume-remote-services.task: From one C# service, call another service's REST API over HTTP by injecting its interface and awaiting typed method calls, instead of building HTTP requests by hand.

abp-api/expose-http-apis.task: Publish every application service in a project as REST endpoints without hand-writing controllers, following the app's routing conventions.

abp-api/use-abp-standard-endpoints.task: Expose an extra server value (like the current deployment version) to all front-end clients by adding it to the data the app already sends clients at startup, instead of adding a new endpoint.

abp-api/version-http-apis.task: Add API versioning so v1 and v2 of the same auto-generated service endpoints can be served side by side.

abp-testing/test-abp-applications.task: Write an integration test that runs a service method under a faked signed-in user and asserts the real authorization pipeline denies the call without the required permission.

abp-testing/test-angular-ui.task: Set up a unit test for an ABP Angular component so its TestBed boots and renders without calling any real backend.

abp-testing/test-mvc-razor-ui.task: Write an in-process HTTP integration test that requests a Razor page and asserts an anonymous request is redirected while a signed-in one returns the page.

abp-ui/angular-ui.task: Add a lazy-loaded feature route to the app's sidebar navigation that is hidden unless the user holds a given permission, and load its data from the backend through a typed client.

abp-ui/blazor-ui.task: Create a Blazor page that greets the current user by name with a localized title and calls a backend service, reusing the framework's injected services rather than injecting them yourself.

abp-ui/build-angular-lists-and-forms.task: Build a server-side paged, sortable, searchable data table bound to a paged backend endpoint, with its subscriptions cleaned up when the component is destroyed.

abp-ui/build-mvc-widgets.task: Build a reusable dashboard component that ships its own JavaScript and CSS so pages rendering it don't have to include those assets themselves, and can be refreshed client-side.

abp-ui/extend-angular-module-ui.task: Add an extra column and a custom row action to the shipped user-management grid, plus an extra field on its create form, without forking the module.

abp-ui/extend-application-shell.task: Inject an analytics script into every page's layout and add a notification bell to the app's top toolbar, without copying the theme's layout file.

abp-ui/menus-and-localization.task: Add a navigation menu item that links to a feature page and shows a label translated for the current culture.

abp-ui/mvc-razor-ui.task: Build a Razor Pages create form shown in a modal dialog using themed form controls, opened from JavaScript.

abp-ui/use-mvc-javascript-apis.task: From page JavaScript, call a backend service to fill a server-side paged data grid and gate its delete action on the current user's permission.
